input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (which aligns with Android deprecating `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`).

Let me provide a concise answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI and Android keyboard handling.

In .NET MAUI / Android, `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated in Android API 30 (Android 11). In .NET 10 MAUI, the recommended approach is to use `WindowInsets` and edge-to-edge handling, or use `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_PAN`, or handle the keyboard insets programmatically.


For .NET 10, I should focus on the practical solutions: using `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Pan` as a quick fix, leveraging Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` API to listen for keyboard insets and adjust the layout accordingly, or switching to edge-to-edge rendering. The most robust approach is setting `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_NOTHING` and manually responding to inset changes through the `WindowInsets` API.
